# Google Play Console Declarations Guide for SensIO

Use these exact answers to complete the permission declaration forms in Google Play Console.

---

## 1. Foreground Service Declarations

### A. Camera Permission (`FOREGROUND_SERVICE_CAMERA`)
* **Task Selected:** `Background camera streaming`
* **Video Link:** *(Upload video or YouTube link generated by `scripts/record_play_store_declaration_video.sh`)*
* **Written Justification:**
  ```text
  SensIO operates as a user-initiated local API server. When the foreground service is active with a persistent ongoing notification, local developer scripts and background AI agents request remote camera snapshots via HTTP POST (/v1/camera/{id}/capture) to provide physical visual input to local LLMs while the user is navigating other apps or when the screen is dimmed.
  ```

---

### B. Location Permission (`FOREGROUND_SERVICE_LOCATION`)
* **Task Selected:** `User-initiated location sharing` / `Background location updates`
* **Video Link:** *(Upload video or YouTube link)*
* **Written Justification:**
  ```text
  SensIO streams high-precision GPS location coordinates over local WebSockets (/v1/stream/location) and REST (/v1/location) to user-configured local AI agents and robotics scripts while running in the background. A persistent ongoing notification remains visible in the system status bar at all times while location streaming is active.
  ```

---

### C. Special Use Permission (`FOREGROUND_SERVICE_SPECIAL_USE`)
* **Task Selected:** `Other`
* **Video Link:** *(Upload video or YouTube link)*
* **Written Justification:**
  ```text
  SensIO runs a continuous local HTTP/WebSocket API server (Ktor on port 8765) and hardware sensor bridge (50Hz IMU, USB host serial, touch tracking, decibel audio levels) serving real-time device telemetry to local developer tools, Termux, and local LLMs (Ollama/HuggingFace). The task must run as an uninterrupted foreground service because local scripts and background AI agents require continuous network connectivity to phone hardware without Android OS Doze or background battery optimization killing WebSocket connections.
  ```

---

## 2. Accessibility Service Declaration

* **Why does your app require AccessibilityServices API?**
  ```text
  SensIO uses the AccessibilityService API to capture touchscreen tap locations (X, Y coordinates) across applications when the user explicitly enables Global Touch Tracking. This allows local developer automation scripts and spatial AI agents on the user's private local network to receive touch interaction signals.
  ```

* **Does your app collect or share sensitive data via AccessibilityService API?**
  ```text
  No. SensIO does not collect, store, transmit, or share any personal or screen data with external servers or third parties. All data remains 100% local on the user's device and private network.
  ```

* **In-App Prominent Disclosure Verification:**
  - SensIO presents an in-app Prominent Disclosure dialog when the user clicks "Enable Global Touch Tracking".
  - The dialog displays full usage explanations and explicit "Agree & Enable" and "Decline" buttons prior to opening system settings.
